title: LLM call inside the polling loop is not deadline-bounded
body_excerpt: `startFirstCurriculumSession` checks `Date.now() <= deadline` only at the top of each while iteration. Once `materializeFocusedBookTopics` is entered, the `generateBookTopics` LLM call runs to comp
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.ts lines 844-858: after `materializeFocusedBookTopics` succeeds, an unconditional `findFirstAvailableTopicId` poll is made immediately (line 852) without re-checking the deadline, bypassing the false-negative failure path. This matches Fix option 1 in the CCR suggestion.

title: jest.config.cjs removes CI tooling added the day before this PR
body_excerpt: The `ciDefaults` block (`silent: true` + `jest-ci-reporter.cjs`) was deliberately added on 2026-05-14 per `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-14-ci-failure-readability-design.md`. This PR removes it wi
evidence: apps/api/jest.config.cjs lines 6-11 and scripts/jest.config.cjs lines 6-11: the `ciDefaults` block with `silent: true` and `jest-ci-reporter.cjs` is present in both configs. The removal mentioned in the CCR has been reverted or was transient.

title: Bare Error thrown in materializeFocusedBookTopics
body_excerpt: `throw new Error('Book not found in this subject')` is an untyped Error. The existing `NotFoundError` (already used inside `persistBookTopics`) is the right class here — it lets the API-boundary cla
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.ts line 414: `throw new Error('Book not found in this subject')` is still a bare `Error`, not `NotFoundError`. `NotFoundError` is imported at line 52 and used at line 460 in `persistBookTopics`, but the ownership-gate throw at line 414 was not updated.
suggested_notion_title: Replace bare Error with NotFoundError in materializeFocusedBookTopics ownership gate
suggested_notion_body: In `apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.ts` line 414, the ownership-gate check in `findFirstAvailableTopicId` throws `new Error('Book not found in this subject')`. This should be `new NotFoundError('Book')` (consistent with line 460 in `persistBookTopics`). A bare Error causes the API-boundary classifier to return a wrong HTTP status. Fix: change line 414 to `throw new NotFoundError('Book');`.

title: GC6 sweep not performed on edited test files
body_excerpt: Editing `library.test.tsx` and `create-subject.test.tsx` triggers the GC6 obligation (CLAUDE.md Code Quality Guards). `library.test.tsx` has 10+ internal `jest.mock('../..')` calls; `create-subject.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/create-subject.test.tsx: three internal relative-path mocks remain without gc1-allow on the `jest.mock(` line — line 46 (`../lib/api-client`), line 50 (`../lib/profile`), and line 110 (`../hooks/use-keyboard-scroll`). `library.test.tsx` has been fully annotated with gc1-allow, but `create-subject.test.tsx` was not swept.
suggested_notion_title: GC6: add gc1-allow annotations to 3 internal jest.mock calls in create-subject.test.tsx
suggested_notion_body: In `apps/mobile/src/app/create-subject.test.tsx`, three internal relative-path `jest.mock` calls lack `gc1-allow` annotations: line 46 (`../lib/api-client`), line 50 (`../lib/profile`), line 110 (`../hooks/use-keyboard-scroll`). CLAUDE.md GC6 requires adding `// gc1-allow: <reason>` on the same line as each `jest.mock(` call (or inside the call as a block comment on the same open-paren line). Also note `format-api-error` at line 101 has the annotation on line 103 (inside the factory), not on the `jest.mock(` line 101.

title: No negative-path tests for the ownership gate in materializeFocusedBookTopics
body_excerpt: The new `session-crud.test.ts` test covers only the success path. Two negative paths are untested: - A `bookId` that belongs to a different profile/subject — A `generateBookTopics` returning zero top
evidence: Grepping apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.test.ts for ownership/IDOR/different-profile/book-not-found negative paths returns no results. Only the success path and deadline-expiry tests exist. Neither (a) a bookId belonging to a different profile/subject nor (b) generateBookTopics returning zero topics are tested.
suggested_notion_title: Add negative-path tests for ownership gate and zero-topics case in session-crud (materializeFocusedBookTopics)
suggested_notion_body: In `apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.test.ts`, two security-boundary negative paths for `materializeFocusedBookTopics` are missing: (1) a bookId that belongs to a different profile/subject — the ownership check at session-crud.ts:408-414 should throw, not proceed; (2) generateBookTopics returning zero topics — should throw `CurriculumSessionNotReadyError`. CLAUDE.md break-test requirement applies to path (1) since it is a security boundary (IDOR). Write one test for each, using the red-green regression pattern.

title: LOOKS GOOD
body_excerpt:
**LOOKS GOOD**

- `isStarterCategoryRefinement` extraction is clean and applied consistently across both handlers.
- `originalInput` added to `onAcceptSuggestion` useCallback deps — the stale-closur
evidence: This is a positive review comment (no actionable finding). No action required.

title: Bug 1 - SSE done-path: close failure incorrectly surfaces as a stream error (database.ts lines 60-63, inline comment posted)
body_excerpt:
Bug 1 - SSE done-path: close failure incorrectly surfaces as a stream error (database.ts lines 60-63, inline comment posted)

When done === true, await closeOnce() sits bare inside the try block. If
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/database.ts lines 61-66: `await closeOnce().catch((closeErr) => { captureException(closeErr, ...) })` — close failure is caught and sent to Sentry, then `controller.close()` is called unconditionally. The SSE cancel path (lines 83-88) also wraps `closeOnce().catch(...)`. Both fixes are implemented. database.test.ts lines 137-165 add the 'close fails after successful SSE consumption' regression test.

title: scratchpad.md` committed with local machine paths
body_excerpt:
**1. `_scratchpad.md` committed with local machine paths**
The file contains `/Users/vetinari/_dev/eduagent-build/...` absolute paths from the planning session. It should not be checked in. Remove i
evidence: `git ls-files | grep scratchpad` returns empty — the file is not tracked. `.gitignore` line 98 has `_scratchpad.md`. The file was removed and gitignored.

title: GC6 sweep not done — `library.test.tsx` (12 internal mocks) and `LearnerScreen.test.tsx` (5 internal mocks)
body_excerpt:
**2. GC6 sweep not done — `library.test.tsx` (12 internal mocks) and `LearnerScreen.test.tsx` (5 internal mocks)**
Both test files were edited in this PR. CLAUDE.md GC6 requires either sweeping rel
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/library.test.tsx: all internal relative-path jest.mock calls (e.g. lines 97-303) now carry `/* gc1-allow: ... */` inline on the `jest.mock(` line. apps/mobile/src/components/home/LearnerScreen.test.tsx: all 5+ internal mocks have gc1-allow annotations on the `jest.mock(` line (lines 68, 74, 79, 116, 123, 130, 141, 148, 171). GC6 sweep complete for both files.

title: eas.json` — production `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` changed to raw Workers URL
body_excerpt:
**3. `eas.json` — production `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` changed to raw Workers URL**
The production endpoint was changed from `https://api.mentomate.com` (vanity domain) to `https://mentomate-api-prd.zwi
evidence: apps/mobile/eas.json line 12: `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` is `https://api.mentomate.com` (the vanity domain). The raw Workers URL is absent from the production profile. The change was reverted.

title: GC6 annotation missing (database.test.ts line 8, inline comment posted)
body_excerpt:
GC6 annotation missing (database.test.ts line 8, inline comment posted)

CLAUDE.md GC6 requires annotating any jest.mock('@eduagent/...') in an edited test file with "// gc1-allow: <reason>" on the
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/database.test.ts line 18: `jest.mock('@eduagent/database', () => mockDatabaseModule.module); // gc1-allow: database middleware unit test needs closeDatabase/createDatabase spies`. The annotation is on the same line as `jest.mock(`. The `../services/sentry` internal mock mentioned in the finding is absent — the test now mocks `@sentry/cloudflare` (external boundary, line 19) instead, which requires no annotation.

title: // gc1-allow: <reason> annotation moved from the same line as jest.mock( to the next line inside the block.
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/quiz.test.ts`
Line: reformatted lines
Issue: `// gc1-allow: <reason>` annotation moved from the same line as `jest.mock(` to the next line inside the block. CLAUDE.md expl
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/quiz.test.ts: all gc1-allow annotations are on the same line as `jest.mock(` — either as a trailing `// gc1-allow:` comment (line 23) or as an inline block comment in the first argument position (lines 29, 42, 58, 75, 87). Pattern complies with CLAUDE.md requirement.

title: Same gc1-allow annotation placement drift as quiz.test.ts
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/settings.test.ts`
Line: reformatted lines
Issue: Same gc1-allow annotation placement drift as quiz.test.ts — annotation moved inside the block instead of on the `jest.mock
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/settings.test.ts lines 24-27: `jest.mock(` is on line 24 and the `gc1-allow` annotation is on line 25 (inside the argument). CLAUDE.md requires the annotation on the SAME LINE as `jest.mock(`. The annotation needs to move to line 24: `jest.mock( // gc1-allow: ...` or the module string must be on the same line. This is distinct from quiz.test.ts which was already fixed.
suggested_notion_title: Fix gc1-allow annotation placement in settings.test.ts (must be on jest.mock( line)
suggested_notion_body: In `apps/api/src/routes/settings.test.ts` lines 24-27, the `jest.mock(` call is split across lines and the `gc1-allow` annotation is on line 25 (the module path line), not line 24 (the `jest.mock(` line). CLAUDE.md § Code Quality Guards requires: 'append `// gc1-allow: <reason>` on the same line as the `jest.mock(` call'. Move the comment or restructure to single-line: `jest.mock('@eduagent/database', () => mockDatabaseModule.module); // gc1-allow: ...`

title: Fallback query fires during initial load (doubles subject API requests)
body_excerpt:
**4. Fallback query fires during initial load (doubles subject API requests)**
`library.tsx` line 166: `enabled: !Array.isArray(subjectsQuery.data)` is `true` while the primary query is still loading
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/library.tsx line 174: `enabled: subjectsQuery.isError && !Array.isArray(subjectsQuery.data)` — the `isError` condition was added, preventing the fallback query from firing during initial load when data is simply undefined.

title: HTTP 500 added to retryable seed statuses
body_excerpt:
**5. HTTP 500 added to retryable seed statuses**
`test-seed.ts` line 23: 502/503 are gateway errors (transient); 500 is an application error (usually a real bug). Retrying 500 up to 6 times masks pe
evidence: apps/mobile/e2e-web/helpers/test-seed.ts line 23: `RETRYABLE_STATUSES = new Set([403, 429, 502, 503])` — 500 is absent. The comment on line 18-20 documents 403/429 as Cloudflare rate-limit codes, consistent with the retryable intent.

title: Sentry DSN silently removed from staging and internal-distribution profiles
body_excerpt:
**6. Sentry DSN silently removed from staging and internal-distribution profiles**
Both staging profiles in `eas.json` had `EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` removed. Staging builds will no longer report error
evidence: apps/mobile/eas.json: `EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` is present in all three profiles — production (line 14), development (line 25), and preview (line 41). The DSN removal was reverted.

title: 48+ eval-LLM snapshots updated without any corresponding prompt file changes
body_excerpt:
**7. 48+ eval-LLM snapshots updated without any corresponding prompt file changes**
The PR updates files in `apps/api/eval-llm/snapshots/probes/` but no `*-prompts.ts` or `services/llm/*.ts` files ar
evidence: Subsequent commits (d323b88a1 'chore(eval): sync prompt snapshots', b657ae578 'feat(eval): add adversarial LLM quality runners') show the snapshots have been actively maintained and re-anchored to current prompt state. The concern was a process question about a specific PR's state; the codebase has since had explicit snapshot-sync commits confirming alignment.

title: GC1 violation in database.test.ts:13-15 — A new `jest.mock("../services/sentry", ...)` was added without a `gc1-allow` annotation.
body_excerpt: 1. GC1 violation in database.test.ts:13-15 — A new `jest.mock("../services/sentry", ...)` was added without a `gc1-allow` annotation. CI GC1 ratchet will fail this. Either add `// gc1-allow: <reas
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/database.test.ts: the `jest.mock('../services/sentry', ...)` call that was flagged no longer exists. Line 19 mocks `@sentry/cloudflare` (external Sentry SDK boundary), not the internal `../services/sentry` wrapper. No GC1 violation remains.

title: DB pool leak when SSE body is already locked (database.ts:40) — If `response.body.getReader()` throws (body locked by another middleware), the exception exits `wrapStreamingResponseForDatabaseClose`
body_excerpt: 2. DB pool leak when SSE body is already locked (database.ts:40) — If `response.body.getReader()` throws (body locked by another middleware), the exception exits `wrapStreamingResponseForDatabaseClos
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/database.ts lines 44-49: `reader = response.body.getReader()` is wrapped in try/catch that returns `false` on failure, causing the caller to fall through to the normal `closeDatabaseWithFallback` path. No pool leak. database.test.ts lines 227-251 has the 'falls back to normal close when an SSE body reader is unavailable' regression test.

title: Non-SSE close errors propagate out of the finally block (database.ts:19-29)
body_excerpt: 3. Non-SSE close errors propagate out of the finally block (database.ts:19-29) — In non-Cloudflare environments (local wrangler dev, Node integration tests), when `executionCtx.waitUntil` throws we f
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/database.ts lines 23-27: `closeDatabase(db).catch((err) => { captureException(err, ...) })` — the close promise has `.catch()` applied before being passed to `waitUntil`, so a rejection is captured by Sentry and swallowed rather than escaping the middleware. database.test.ts lines 253-272 covers the 'reports close failures after a non-streaming response' case.
